design_philosophy: Invisible Design. The interface should feel like a native extension of the OS. No decorative gradients, no startup illustrations, no 'delight' animations that slow down the user. Speed and Clarity are the only metrics.

layout.container: max-w-md mx-auto min-h-screen bg-[#F2F2F7] overflow-hidden relative
layout.structure: Single view app. The 'Input' is the default state. 'Results' slide up or appear instantly below. No complex navigation bars.

inputs.style: h-12 bg-[#E5E5EA] rounded-[12px] px-4 text-[17px] text-black placeholder:text-[#8E8E93] border-none focus:ring-0 focus:bg-[#D1D1D6] transition-colors

buttons.primary: h-12 bg-[#007AFF] hover:bg-[#0062CC] text-white text-[17px] font-semibold rounded-[12px] w-full shadow-none active:opacity-80 transition-opacity
buttons.secondary: h-12 bg-[#E5E5EA] text-[#007AFF] text-[17px] font-semibold rounded-[12px] w-full active:bg-[#D1D1D6]

instructions_to_main_agent: 1. FORCE LIGHT MODE: Ensure 'dark' class is never applied to the html/body tag. The app must be strictly light mode.
2. IOS INPUTS: Inputs should feel like iOS Spotlight search. Grey background, no border, clear text.
3. NO GRADIENTS: Do not use gradients for buttons or backgrounds. Solid colors only.
4. BOTTOM SHEET FEEL: If possible, make the 'Results' section feel like a sheet sliding up over the map/input area.
5. FONT SIZING: Use 16px+ for inputs to prevent iOS Safari from zooming in on focus.
6. DATA-TESTID: Add data-testid to all inputs (pickup-input, dest-input) and buttons (compare-btn, uber-btn, lyft-btn).
7. MAP FALLBACK: Use the 'map_bg' asset as a subtle background image for the main container if no interactive map is present, with a white overlay (opacity 90%) to ensure text readability.
8. SAVED ROUTE: The 'Weekend Ride' button should look like a 'Favorite' shortcut in Apple Maps (Circle icon + Label below, or a compact row).
universal_guidelines: Avoid 'AI Slop': No generic centered layouts. Use the 'Grouped List' aesthetic of iOS Settings.
Micro-interactions: Buttons should have a distinct 'active' state (opacity reduction or scale down) to mimic native touch.
Spacing: Use 'p-4' as the standard edge padding. Use 'gap-3' for lists.
Shadows: Use very subtle shadows 'shadow-sm' or none. iOS relies on background color contrast (White on Light Grey) more than heavy shadows.
